source: Smelling Salts Review — extracted from conversation history line 81 (GitHub Innovation Master List)
name: The Puppet Strings Report — Financial Transparency Tool
description: The Puppet Strings Report is a transparency tool that shows Ruby Red exactly what is happening behind the scenes in her financial life. Who is charging her what. Where the hidden fees are. What her data is being used for by banks, phone companies, and tech platforms. It pulls back the curtain on the greedy capitalist world and gives Ruby the information she needs to fight back. This is part of the Protection Layer — Maven as a shield, not just a tool.
north_star_alignment: Ruby Red is being manipulated by systems she cannot see. Dynamic pricing charges her more because of her postal code. Hidden fees are buried in fine print she does not have time to read. Her data is sold to predatory lenders who target her with payday loan ads. The Puppet Strings Report makes the invisible visible.

protection_layer_context.description: The Protection Layer is Maven's shield function — protecting Ruby from the three gangster worlds. The Puppet Strings Report is one of two tools in this layer.
protection_layer_context.tool_1: Puppet Strings Report — shows Ruby what's happening behind the scenes
protection_layer_context.tool_2: Bullshit Detector — flags predatory offers, scams, and unfair terms before Ruby signs anything

report_sections.description: Every fee Ruby has been charged in the last 90 days, categorized by type (NSF, maintenance, service, penalty), with comparison to what she SHOULD be paying
report_sections.data_source: Budget Builder, Fee Fighter, bank transaction data

report_sections.description: A list of every company that has accessed Ruby's data in the last 90 days — credit checks, data broker sales, ad targeting
report_sections.data_source: Credit bureau reports, data broker registries, ad platform transparency tools

report_sections.section: The Price You Pay vs. The Price They Pay
report_sections.description: Comparison of what Ruby pays for common services (phone, internet, insurance) vs. what someone in a wealthier postal code pays for the same service
report_sections.data_source: Maven Data Cooperative aggregated pricing data

report_sections.description: Ads and offers Ruby has been targeted with that are predatory — payday loans, high-interest credit cards, rent-to-own schemes
report_sections.data_source: Ad transparency tools, Maven's Bullshit Detector

report_sections.description: What Ruby's data is worth to the companies harvesting it, and how the Maven Data Cooperative is using the same data to benefit her village instead

grace_delivery.frequency: Monthly — Grace presents the report in a warm, digestible conversation
grace_delivery.tone: Not scary. Not overwhelming. Grace walks Ruby through it like a friend explaining something important over coffee.
grace_delivery.action_items: Every section ends with: 'Here's what we can do about this.' Grace connects each finding to a Maven action — cancel a fee, block a tracker, switch a plan.

connection_to_other_features.maven_data_cooperative: The Puppet Strings Report uses cooperative data for the pricing comparison section
connection_to_other_features.bullshit_detector: The Bullshit Detector feeds the Predatory Targeting section
connection_to_other_features.vampire_slayer: Hidden subscriptions found by Vampire Slayer appear in the Hidden Fees section
connection_to_other_features.fee_fighter: Fee Fighter results feed the Hidden Fees section
connection_to_other_features.dignity_score: Each Puppet Strings action taken improves Ruby's Dignity Score

build_complexity: HIGH — requires data aggregation from multiple sources, credit bureau integration, ad transparency API access
